problem_statement: We need to evaluate whether to build our own ML inference platform or buy a managed service, considering our 6-month deadline and limited DevOps team.

recommended_approach.objective: Evaluate whether to build our own ML inference platform or buy a managed service within a 6-month deadline.

steps.description: Identify and evaluate available managed ML inference services that meet our requirements.
Evaluate the current skills and resources of the DevOps team to determine if building an in-house solution is feasible.
Gather and document the technical and business requirements for the ML inference platform, whether built or bought.
Analyze the total cost of ownership for both building an in-house solution and using a managed service.
Estimate the time required to build an in-house platform versus the time to implement a managed service.
Identify potential risks associated with both options, including technical, operational, and financial risks.
Based on the findings from the previous steps, decide whether to build or buy the ML inference platform.

assumptions: The DevOps team has the necessary skills to evaluate both options.
There are sufficient budgetary resources to conduct the analysis.
The timeline of 6 months is realistic for the decision-making process.
risks: Market options may not meet our specific requirements.
Internal capabilities may be overestimated, leading to project failure.
Cost estimates may be inaccurate, affecting the decision.
Time constraints may limit thorough evaluation.
